pages.text:
01_master_services_agreement
MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT
This Master Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into on 1 August 2025 (the "Effective
Date")
BETWEEN:
Acme Corp, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, having its registered office
at 14 Residency Road, Bengaluru 560025 (the "Client");
AND
BuildrCo Pvt Ltd, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, having its registered
office at 7 Outer Ring Road, Bengaluru 560103 (the "Vendor").
The Client and the Vendor are referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties".
RECITALS
WHEREAS the Client wishes to procure the design, development, and deployment of a customer
onboarding platform; and WHEREAS the Vendor represents that it has the expertise, personnel,
and capacity to provide such services; NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual
covenants set out below, the Parties agree as follows.
1. DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION
1.1 In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires:
"Statement of Work" or "SOW" means a document executed by the Parties under this Agreement
describing specific Services, Deliverables, timelines, and fees.
"Deliverables" means the items, materials, software, and outputs to be provided by the Vendor as
specified in a SOW.
"Change Order" means a written instrument executed by both Parties amending an SOW or this
Agreement pursuant to clause 5.
"Background IP" means intellectual property owned by or licensed to a Party prior to the Effective
Date or developed independently of this Agreement.
"Confidential Information" means non-public information disclosed by one Party to the other that
is marked confidential or would reasonably be understood to be confidential.
1.2 Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation. References to clauses are
to clauses of this Agreement unless stated otherwise. In the event of conflict between this
Agreement and an SOW, this Agreement prevails unless the SOW expressly states otherwise.
2. ENGAGEMENT AND SCOPE

lines.text: 01_master_services_agreement
MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT
This Master Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into on 1 August 2025 (the "Effective
Date")
BETWEEN:
Acme Corp, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, having its registered office
at 14 Residency Road, Bengaluru 560025 (the "Client");
AND
BuildrCo Pvt Ltd, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, having its registered
office at 7 Outer Ring Road, Bengaluru 560103 (the "Vendor").
The Client and the Vendor are referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties".
RECITALS
WHEREAS the Client wishes to procure the design, development, and deployment of a customer
onboarding platform; and WHEREAS the Vendor represents that it has the expertise, personnel,
and capacity to provide such services; NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual
covenants set out below, the Parties agree as follows.
1. DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION
1.1 In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires:
"Statement of Work" or "SOW" means a document executed by the Parties under this Agreement
describing specific Services, Deliverables, timelines, and fees.
"Deliverables" means the items, materials, software, and outputs to be provided by the Vendor as
specified in a SOW.
"Change Order" means a written instrument executed by both Parties amending an SOW or this
Agreement pursuant to clause 5.
"Background IP" means intellectual property owned by or licensed to a Party prior to the Effective
Date or developed independently of this Agreement.
"Confidential Information" means non-public information disclosed by one Party to the other that
is marked confidential or would reasonably be understood to be confidential.
1.2 Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation. References to clauses are
to clauses of this Agreement unless stated otherwise. In the event of conflict between this
Agreement and an SOW, this Agreement prevails unless the SOW expressly states otherwise.
2. ENGAGEMENT AND SCOPE

pages.text:
2.1 The Client engages the Vendor to provide the services described in one or more SOWs (the
"Services"). The Vendor's Project Manager, Ms. R. Iyer, shall be the single point of contact for
delivery.
2.2 Each SOW forms part of this Agreement upon execution by both Parties. The first SOW (SOW-
01) is executed concurrently with this Agreement.
2.3 The Vendor shall perform the Services with reasonable skill and care and in accordance with
good industry practice.
3. TERM
3.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until the completion of all
SOWs, unless terminated earlier in accordance with clause 11.
4. FEES AND PAYMENT
4.1 The total fee for SOW-01 is INR 48,00,000 (Indian Rupees Forty-Eight Lakh), payable in three
milestones as set out below.
4.2 Milestone 1 — INR 16,00,000, payable on signing of SOW-01. Payment authorisation: the
Client's Head of Procurement.
4.3 Milestone 2 — INR 16,00,000, payable on completion of the Platform Build Deliverable.
4.4 Milestone 3 — INR 16,00,000, payable on UAT Sign-Off. Payment authorisation: the Client's
Chief Financial Officer.
4.5 Invoices are payable within 30 (thirty) days of receipt. Late payments accrue interest at 1% per
month.
4.6 All fees are exclusive of applicable taxes, which shall be borne by the Client.
5. CHANGE CONTROL
5.1 Either Party may request a change to the scope, Deliverables, or timeline of an SOW. No
change is binding unless documented in a Change Order signed by authorised representatives of
both Parties.
5.2 The Vendor shall, on request, provide a written impact assessment (cost, schedule, and risk)
for any proposed change before a Change Order is executed.
6. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
6.1 Each Party retains all right, title, and interest in its Background IP. Nothing in this Agreement
transfers ownership of a Party's Background IP to the other.
6.2 The Vendor grants the Client a non-exclusive licence to use the Vendor's Background IP solely
to the extent necessary for the Client to use the Deliverables.

5.1 Either Party may request a change to the scope, Deliverables, or timeline of an SOW. No
change is binding unless documented in a Change Order signed by authorised representatives of
both Parties.
5.2 The Vendor shall, on request, provide a written impact assessment (cost, schedule, and risk)
for any proposed change before a Change Order is executed.
6. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
6.1 Each Party retains all right, title, and interest in its Background IP. Nothing in this Agreement
transfers ownership of a Party's Background IP to the other.
6.2 The Vendor grants the Client a non-exclusive licence to use the Vendor's Background IP solely

pages.text:
6.3 The Client shall not reverse engineer or sublicense the Vendor's Background IP except as
permitted by law.
7. WARRANTIES
7.1 The Vendor warrants that the Services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike
manner and that the Deliverables will materially conform to the specifications in the relevant SOW
for a period of 90 days following acceptance.
7.2 Except as expressly stated, the Vendor disclaims all other warranties to the extent permitted by
law.
8. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
8.1 Subject to clause 8.2, each Party's aggregate liability under this Agreement is limited to the
total fees paid under the relevant SOW in the 12 months preceding the claim.
8.2 Nothing limits liability for fraud, wilful misconduct, or any liability that cannot be limited by law.
9. CONFIDENTIALITY
9.1 Each Party shall keep the other's Confidential Information secret during the term and for two
years after termination, and shall use it only for the purposes of this Agreement.
9.2 Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information that is public through no fault of the
receiving Party, or independently developed without reference to the disclosing Party's information.
10. DATA PROTECTION
10.1 Each Party shall comply with applicable laws in performing this Agreement.
11. TERMINATION
11.1 Either Party may terminate this Agreement or any SOW for material breach not cured within
30 days of written notice.
11.2 The Client may terminate any SOW for convenience on 60 days' written notice, in which case
the Vendor is paid for Services performed up to the termination date.
12. GOVERNING LAW AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION
12.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of India.
12.2 Any dispute shall be referred to arbitration in Bengaluru under the Arbitration and Conciliation
Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual agreement.
13. GENERAL
13.1 This Agreement, together with its SOWs and Change Orders, constitutes the entire
agreement between the Parties and supersedes all prior discussions.
13.2 No variation is effective unless in writing and signed by both Parties.

pages.text:
02_statement_of_work
STATEMENT OF WORK — SOW-01
Customer Onboarding Platform
This Statement of Work ("SOW-01") is issued under and governed by the Master Services
Agreement dated 1 August 2025 between Acme Corp ("Client") and BuildrCo Pvt Ltd ("Vendor").
Capitalised terms not defined here have the meanings given in the Master Services Agreement. In
the event of conflict, the Master Services Agreement prevails unless this SOW expressly states
otherwise.
1. BACKGROUND AND OBJECTIVES
1.1 The Client requires a customer onboarding platform to replace its existing manual onboarding
process. The platform will capture customer records, validate documentation, and integrate with
the Client's downstream systems.
1.2 This SOW sets out the Deliverables, acceptance criteria, timeline, and dependencies for the
engagement.
2. DELIVERABLES
2.1 Platform Build — the onboarding web application, comprising the customer registration
workflow, document upload, and an administrative console. Acceptance: passes the agreed test
plan set out in Appendix A and obtains Client UAT Sign-Off.
2.2 Data Migration — migration of existing customer records from the Client's legacy system into
the new platform, including data cleansing and validation. Acceptance: a reconciliation report
demonstrating 100% record parity, approved by the Client's Data Lead.
2.3 Performance Tuning — optimisation of platform response times prior to go-live.
2.4 UAT Sign-Off — formal Client acceptance of the platform following user acceptance testing.
2.5 Hypercare Support — 30 days of post go-live support, including defect triage and resolution,
commencing on the go-live date.
3. TIMELINE
3.1 Project start: 1 August 2025.
3.2 Platform Build complete by 15 September 2025.
3.3 UAT Sign-Off to be obtained by 30 September 2025.
3.4 Target go-live: 5 October 2025.
4. RESPONSIBILITIES

lines.text: 02_statement_of_work
STATEMENT OF WORK — SOW-01
Customer Onboarding Platform
This Statement of Work ("SOW-01") is issued under and governed by the Master Services
Agreement dated 1 August 2025 between Acme Corp ("Client") and BuildrCo Pvt Ltd ("Vendor").
Capitalised terms not defined here have the meanings given in the Master Services Agreement. In
the event of conflict, the Master Services Agreement prevails unless this SOW expressly states
otherwise.
1. BACKGROUND AND OBJECTIVES
1.1 The Client requires a customer onboarding platform to replace its existing manual onboarding
process. The platform will capture customer records, validate documentation, and integrate with
the Client's downstream systems.
1.2 This SOW sets out the Deliverables, acceptance criteria, timeline, and dependencies for the
engagement.
2. DELIVERABLES
2.1 Platform Build — the onboarding web application, comprising the customer registration
workflow, document upload, and an administrative console. Acceptance: passes the agreed test
plan set out in Appendix A and obtains Client UAT Sign-Off.
2.2 Data Migration — migration of existing customer records from the Client's legacy system into
the new platform, including data cleansing and validation. Acceptance: a reconciliation report
demonstrating 100% record parity, approved by the Client's Data Lead.
2.3 Performance Tuning — optimisation of platform response times prior to go-live.
2.4 UAT Sign-Off — formal Client acceptance of the platform following user acceptance testing.
2.5 Hypercare Support — 30 days of post go-live support, including defect triage and resolution,
commencing on the go-live date.
3. TIMELINE
3.1 Project start: 1 August 2025.
3.2 Platform Build complete by 15 September 2025.
3.3 UAT Sign-Off to be obtained by 30 September 2025.
3.4 Target go-live: 5 October 2025.
4. RESPONSIBILITIES

pages.text:
4.1 The Vendor is responsible for the design, build, and testing of the Platform Build Deliverable
(clause 2.1) and for Performance Tuning (clause 2.3).
4.2 The Client is responsible for providing timely access to the legacy system, nominating UAT
participants, and providing test data by 20 August 2025.
5. DEPENDENCIES
5.1 Data Migration (clause 2.2) is a prerequisite for UAT and must be completed before UAT can
begin. Data Migration is scheduled to complete by 15 October 2025.
5.2 The Platform Build depends on the Client confirming the final data schema by 25 August 2025.
6. ACCEPTANCE PROCEDURE
6.1 On submission of a Deliverable, the Client has 10 business days to review and either accept it
or provide written notice of non-conformities.
6.2 If the Client does not respond within the review period, the Deliverable is deemed accepted.
6.3 The Vendor shall remedy notified non-conformities within 10 business days and resubmit for
review.
7. FEES
7.1 Fees for this SOW are as set out in clause 4 of the Master Services Agreement (total INR
48,00,000 across three milestones).
7.2 Expenses pre-approved in writing by the Client are reimbursed at cost.
8. KEY PERSONNEL
8.1 The Vendor's Project Manager for this SOW is Ms. R. Iyer.
8.2 The Vendor shall not reassign key personnel without the Client's prior written consent, such
consent not to be unreasonably withheld.
APPENDIX A — TEST PLAN
[To be attached.]

pages.text:
03_change_order
CHANGE ORDER — CO-01
Amendment to SOW-01 (Customer Onboarding Platform)
This Change Order ("CO-01") is issued under clause 5 of the Master Services Agreement dated 1
August 2025 and amends Statement of Work SOW-01 between Acme Corp ("Client") and BuildrCo
Pvt Ltd ("Vendor"). Capitalised terms have the meanings given in the Master Services Agreement
and SOW-01. Except as amended here, SOW-01 remains in full force.
Date of this Change Order: 5 September 2025
1. REASON FOR CHANGE
1.1 Following a strategic review, the Client wishes to (a) accelerate go-live to capture an upcoming
campaign window, and (b) add a third-party CRM integration to the scope.
2. CHANGES TO SCOPE
2.1 A new Deliverable is added to SOW-01:
2.1.1 CRM Integration — a real-time integration between the onboarding platform and the Client's
Salesforce CRM, including field mapping and error handling. Acceptance: passes an integration
test demonstrating successful record sync across all mapped fields.
3. CHANGES TO TIMELINE
3.1 The target go-live date in clause 3.4 of SOW-01 is brought forward from 5 October 2025 to 25
September 2025.
3.2 All other dates in SOW-01, including the Data Migration completion date, remain unchanged
unless expressly amended here.
4. CHANGES TO FEES
4.1 The additional fee for the CRM Integration Deliverable is INR 8,00,000, payable on acceptance
of that Deliverable.
4.2 This additional fee is added to the total contract value, bringing it to INR 56,00,000.
5. EFFECT
5.1 This Change Order, once signed by both Parties, forms part of SOW-01 and the Master
Services Agreement.
SIGNED for and on behalf of the Parties:
For Acme Corp: ___ Name: __ Title: __
